title: Veterinary Technician

rationales.taskAutomation: Charting, standard lab processing and client-reminder communication carry a meaningful digital share; animal handling and hands-on clinical support are not automatable.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI tools targeting veterinary tech workflows are early and mostly generic, borrowed from human lab-automation products.
rationales.adoption: Adoption is concentrated in larger corporate veterinary chains; most independent clinics use little beyond practice-management software.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can handle appointment reminders and routine chart entries, but restraint, sample collection and hands-on animal care stay physical.

tasksGrowing: Animal restraint and hands-on clinical support
salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 29-2056 (Veterinary Technologists and Technicians), p25-p75 rounded
insight: Veterinary tech work is hands-on animal care with a modest paperwork layer around it, and AI tooling for this specific role is still immature. Corporate consolidation and staffing shortages are bigger near-term forces than automation.
